def: s.m. The nectar conferring immortality; ambrosia, the drink of the gods, the water of life; anything sweet; any pleasant drink; a guava; immortality; final emancipation
more: n. m. S. अमृत from अ not, मृ to die. 1. Ambrosia; nectar; the drink of the gods; the water of life ( āb-i-hayāt ). Nām imrit, pilāë bis . Prov. Administered a poison, nectar called. 2. Any pleasant drink; a cordial. imrat bānī , n. f. Honeyed words; rapturous strains; Syren voice. Dasrath sahit Ajodhyā-bāsī, bolat imrit-bānī . Rāmāyan. imrat kā chhīṅṭā denā , v. a. To sprinkle with the water of life; bring to life. Māno merā kahā, pȳā, amrit kā chhīṭā dijye, Merī khusī, Makārāj, āj is jaṅgal meṅ kījye . Play. Rūp Basaṅt.
(vulg. imrat ) s. m. The food of the gods, ambrosia, nectar, the water of life, what is immortal or renders immortal, any thing sweet, water, wine, a guava.
subs: imrat-bān (amr̤it+vān), s.m. A variety of the plantain (kelā); a glazed or china jar ( = martabān)
v.t. To sprinkle with the water of life, to restore to life.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अमृत (amṛta). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi امرت (amrt).

ety: S. अयन, अयान; and cf. मान and आज्ञा; Prk. आणा or आना
def: s.f. Course, way, manner, mode ( = andāz, ḍhang, t̤aur); natural disposition or temperament; habit, peculiarity; way or manner of a belle or a coquette, gracefulness, grace, elegance, charm, blandishment ( = adā); affectation; bashfulness, modesty; conceit, pride; will, pleasure, wish; rank, dignity, respectability, reputation, character; proper spirit, self-respect; established rule or custom; vow, oath; promise; interdicted thing; objection, scruple; hindrance, prohibition
more: n. f. Expression; grace; charm; fascination; blandishment. 1. Zumurrud kā moṅḍhā chaman par bichhā. Woh baīṭhī ajab ān se dil-rubā . Mir Hasan. Upon a stool, with emeralds in a garden set, She sat with wondrous grace and blandishment well met. 2. Adā bhī chulbulī, aur ān meṅ bhī kuchh ajab chhalbal, Fusūṅgar aṅkhṛyāṅ zālim kī, aur jis par lagā kājal . Nazīr. A restless tripping grace, a wondrous luring look besides, A witching eye, to which the cruel one lamp-black applies.

n. f. Point of time; second; moment; instant. ān kī ān meṅ, ānan fānan meṅ , adv. In a second; in a moment; in a trice. Phr. In the twinkling of an eye. Garaz, le gaī ān kī ān meṅ, Uṛā-kar woh usko parastān meṅ . Mīr Hasan. In fine, all in the twinkling of an eye, He flew with her to fairy land perdye.

n. f. 1. Desire; wish; craving. Jo aur kisī kī jāṅ ba/?/khshe, to uskī bhī Haq jān rakhe, Jo aur kisī kī ān rakhe, to uskī bhī Haq ān rakhe . Nazīr. Who spares another's life, may God his life preserve. Who grants another's wish, may God his heart's wish serve. 2. Will; pleasure; determination. Terī ān, yā tere gusaīyāṅ kī? Prov. Your will or mine—your master? 3. Obstinacy; stubbornness; wilfulness. Woh apnī ān na chhoṛega, tum apnī bān kyoṅ chhoṛo? He will never give over his ways, why should you give up yours? 4. An avowed refusal or denial; abjuration; renunciation; prohibition. 1. Tujhe kyā mere ghar āne kī ān hai? Wom. 2. Hamāre hāṅ, jī, harī chūṛȳoṅ kī ān hai? Wom. Green bangles are not allowed in our house. 5. Pride; vanity; conceitedness. Apnī ān meṅ marī hī jātī hai . Wom. 6. Rank; degree; dignity; respectability. Muflis kī kuchh nazar nahīṅ rahtī hai ān par, Detā hai apnī jān …
1. ( अन्य anya ) adj. Other, different. 2. (for आज्ञा ) s. f. Order, command.
s. f. 1. Bashfulness, modesty, shame. 2. An oath.
s. f. A graceful attitude, an affected gait; affectation, pride, dignity, proper spirit.
subs: s.f. Way, fashion; habit, peculiarity; grace, elegance, beauty; pride, conceit; spirit, proper pride, dignity

r: an jal, an pān, an pānī
def: n. m. Food and drink. See āb dānā. Sājan chale pardes, sakhī, mujhe an pān kuchh nahīṅ bhātā . Song. Lāonī. My husband gone from me, my dear, nor food nor drink doth please me now. an-jal bikhar'nā , v. n. To be scattered or spread (one's pre-ordained food); to have one's portion of food in the world. Aë pyāre jī, ham ko be-shak khāë qazā jo mhārī āī, Jo an jal rahā bikhar, Pirbhū deṅ hameṅ bachāī . Play. Rūp Basaṅt. My friend, 'tis certain I must die, if such be Heaven's decree, But Heaven spare me if food somewhere dispersed there be for me. an-jal pān kar'nā , v. n. To eat; dine; take one's food. Sabhā bhāṭan āī, rājā, terī, do mujh ko kuchh dān, Bin dīë, likhā nahīṅ karnā an jal pān . Play. Chaubolā . The bard is come, O King, your alms on me bestow, Not lawful 'tis to dine before you've given enow. an-jāl na kar'nā , v. a. Not to eat, take food, or dine. An jal kīnā nāīṅ, ajī, ban-khaṅd meṅ dekho āë haiṅ, Rājā kā amal chhoṛā pīchhe, āge qadam baṛhāe haiṅ . Play. Rūp Basaṅt. Not eaten, nor drunk, the exiles doomed to range the forest o'er, The king's dominions left behind, they journey on before. an-dā'tā, an-dā'yak , n. m. 1. Supporter; cherisher; benefactor; employer. 2. Lord; master; patron. an-dhakel' , n. lit . That which pushes down the food. Fac. Water. an-dhan , n. m. Food and wealth; corn and cattle; competency; easy circumstances. An-dhan anek dhan; sonā, rūpā katek dhan . Prov. Wealth in grain's wealth in the main, silver and gold are wealth in vain.

A negative or privative prefix ( = अ a), corresponding to the English prefixes un-, in-. (In words purely Sanskrit it is prefixed only to those beginning with a vowel; but in Hindī its use is not so restricted, as will be seen from the numerous examples that follow.)
more: A negative prefix equivalent to our Eng. in and un , S. अ (not), which becomes an before a vowel.

n. m. S. अन्न from अद् to eat. 1. Grain; corn; bread; food. 1. Bābū, sunat bāt Sarwan ghabrāë, Ek haṅṛyā, dū an kaise āë . Ballad. Sarwan. Dear Sir, Sarwan at this was troubled sore, One pot—how came of messes two a store! 2. Pūrab des se āī tiryā, ann khāë, pānī kī kiryā . Riddle Ans. ghun a weevil. A female from the East lives on grain, but against water sworn. 2. Sustenance; nourishment; nutriment; subsistence. 1. Sardī kā mārā panpe, an kā mārā na panpe . Prov. The frozen out revives, the starvling droops and dies. 2. An gaële, dhan gaële, sāṅchal joban gaële . Mag. Song. Gone food, gone wealth, gone a perfect bust!

pro. S. अन्य others. Another's; of another. 1 An-kar sir kaddū barābar . Prov. Another's head is a pumpkin. (You may cut it off). 2. An kar khetī, an-kar gāë, Woh pāpī jo māran jāe . Prov. Another's field, another's ox, the sinner on the head the poacher knocks. 3. Ghar kā jogī jognā, aur an gāon kā siddh . Prov. Your town jogi's a smaller breed, a jogi from another town's a jogi great indeed. 4. An ke dhan par Lachhmī Narāyn . Prov. He squats down on another's wealth, and lo! he is Lachhmī Narāyn! 5. Apnī ṭeṭaṛ na nihāre, an kī phullī . Prov. He cannot see the cataract in his own eye, but he sees the sty in another's.

A prefix of negation, privation or diminution, used properly before words beginning with a vowel only; ا or अ being adopted before consonants: in Hindī, however, ان frequently occurs with consonants. So اننت an-ant, اندیکها an-dekhā.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀡- (aṇa-), from Sanskrit अन्- (an-), from Proto-Indo-European *n̥- (“not, un-”).

ety: S. आणकः, or अणकः, 'inferior, small'; rt. अण्
def: s.m. A copper coin, the sixteenth part of a rupee; a measure of land equal to 1⁄640th of an acre; a sixteenth part or share in a business or property. (The term ānā is also used in estimating quality, character, and ability, — much as the expression 'Sixteen ounces to the pound' is used in England; — thus, a man is said to be about 12 ānās — i.e. as not quite up to the mark)
more: v. n. S. आगम् to come. 1. To come; approach; come or draw near. 1. Zahr lagtā hai dugānā, mūjhe jānā terā, Hāṅ, jo bhātā hai to bhātā hai yeh ānā terā . Wom. Rangīn. 2. Kauṅ des kā āonā aur kaun des ko jāë? Rastā paṛā hai dūr kā, pāni pitā jāë . Dohā. From whence, O stranger, do you come, and whither would you go? The way is long: a cup of water take before you go? 3. Bin maut, saīyāṅ mo ko māro chāhat ho, So bin āī ham kaise mar jāëṅ, Mo se ber ber g/?/ri sunī nahīṅ jāëṅ . Rus. Wom. Song. Mine hour not come, my husband, you would strike me dead! Till death itself appear, how bid my spirit fled! How bear your oft-repeated railings on my head! 4. Āī to ramāi, nahīṅ faqat charpāī . Prov. If she come, welcome; if not, my lonely couch welcome! 5. Āë, to jāë kahāṅ . Prov. When it comes, where will it go. (Passion must have its discharge, as clouds must rain). 2. To arrive; reach; attain; come…
1. ( आ and अयन A going) v. n. To come, to be, to become; to be practicable or possible: coming. آن پڙنا ān paṛnā, To happen. 2. s. m. The sixteenth part of a rupee.
subs: s.m. A fee or levy of one anna per rupee.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀆𑀯𑁂𑀇 (āvei), from Sanskrit आपयति (āpayati), from आप् (āp) + -अयति (-ayati). The perfective form آیا (āyā) may be derived from Sanskrit आगत (ā́gata, “arrived”).

def: v.n. To come; to approach; to arrive, reach (to), attain (to); to be acquired; to know how, to be able ( = Fr. savoir); to come to pass, happen, betide, befall; to be or become; to appear; to grow, increase, swelled be inflamed; to rise, to overflow; to come (on, -meṅ), to fit; to be bent or set (upon, -par); to come on, come forth, to pass away, to pass (as urine, semen, &c.)

v.n. To come or draw near, to come (to), arrive (at)
v.n. To come, &c. ( = ānā); to come, or appear, suddenly or unexpectedly: ā-rahnā, ān-rahnā, v.n. To fall or come down (from, -se); to come and reside or dwell (in, -meṅ)
v.n. To fall down ( = gir paṛnā); to fall (upon); to press, throng, make a rush (at or upon)
v.n. To be close upon, be close at hand; to beset, to fall (upon), lie in wait for

(-par), To happen (to), to be afflicted (by), be overtaken (by)

v.n. To chance to come, to come suddenly or unexpectedly, to turn up. (The root of ānā, in the sense of 'to happen,' and sometimes of 'with,' is commonly prefixed to other neuter verbs of motion. Compare with this use of it that of the French verb venir 'to come.' Ánā is sometimes added to a verbal base to intensify it, e.g. uṭh-ānā, nikāl-ānā, To rise suddenly; to escape away, to come right out. It is also used to form statical verbs: e.g. rote ānā, 'to come weeping.')
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀆𑀯𑁂𑀇 (āvei), from Sanskrit आपयति (āpayati), from आप् (āp) + -अयति (-ayati). The perfective form آیا (āyā) may be derived from Sanskrit आगत (ā́gata, “arrived”).

def: n. Coming and going; visiting; interchange of visits; communication; intercourse; access ( āmad o raft ). Qadr kho detā hai har bār kā ānā jānā . Much visiting lowers esteem. ā-paṛnā, ān-paṛnā , v. n. 1. To come or fall upon; come down; descend; drop down on. Kabūtar pe bahrī ān paṛī . 2. To befall; fall out; betide. Tum pe kyā ā-paṛī jo udhār khāte ho? 3. To fall to one's lot; come to (without effort); to fall or descend. Unke ghar meṅ to gaib se daulat ā paṛī . 4. To fall upon; fly at; make a raiḍ ( dhāvā k .). Phr. To make a rush at; bear down upon; ride full tilt against. Jāttriyoṅ par chor ā paṛe . 5. To fall on one's shoulders; to become responsible for. Sārā kām mujhī par ā-paṛā . 6. To prostrate oneself; do homage; worship. Nāth, mohe ab apnā kar līje, A-paṛā hūṅ saran tihārī khabar līje . Bhajan. Lord! make me as thine own, to thee For refuge come. Oh, look on me! ā pauṅhch'nā , v. a. 1. To come or draw near; come to or at; arrive. Tum bhī ā pauṅhche! 2. To approach one's end. Uskī ā pauṅhchī . Prov. His time has come.

Andhrā gurū, bahrā chelā; māṅge haṛ, de baheṛā . Prov. A blind teacher, a deaf disciple; asks for haṛ , receives baheṛā (gall nuts). 5. Andhī pīse, kuttā khāë . Prov.
